title.zh: 卷八十二 王商史丹傅喜傳
title.en: Volume 82: Wang Shang, Shi Dan and Fu Xi
title.raw: 卷八十二 王商史丹傅喜傳 Volume 82: Wang Shang, Shi Dan and Fu Xi

sentences.zh: 卷八十二 王商史丹傅喜傳第五十二

translations.literal: Volume 82: Biographies of Wang Shang, Shi Dan, and Fu Xi, Number 52.
translations.idiomatic: Volume 82: Biographies of Wang Shang, Shi Dan, and Fu Xi (the fifty-second chapter).

sentences.zh: 王商字子威，涿郡蠡吾人也，徙杜陵。

translations.literal: Wang Shang, courtesy name Ziwei, was a man of Liwu in Zhuo Commandery, and his family moved to Duling.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Shang, whose courtesy name was Ziwei, came from Liwu in Zhuo Commandery; the household later relocated to Duling.

sentences.zh: 商公武、武兄無故，皆以宣帝舅封。

translations.literal: Shang's father was Wu; Wu's elder brother was Wugu; both were enfeoffed because they were uncles on the mother's side of Emperor Xuan.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Shang's father was Wang Wu, and Wu's elder brother Wang Wugu; both received marquisates as maternal uncles of Emperor Xuan.

sentences.zh: 無故為平昌侯，武為樂昌侯。

translations.literal: Wugu became Marquis of Pingchang, and Wu became Marquis of Lechang.
translations.idiomatic: Wugu was made Marquis of Pingchang, while Wu became Marquis of Lechang.

sentences.zh: 語在《外戚傳》。

translations.literal: The account is in the 'Biographies of the Consort Families.'
translations.idiomatic: The fuller story appears in the chapter on the imperial in-laws.

sentences.zh: 元帝崩，成帝即位，甚敬重商，徙為左將軍。

translations.literal: When Emperor Yuan died, Emperor Cheng ascended the throne; he greatly respected Wang Shang and transferred him to be General of the Left.
translations.idiomatic: After Emperor Yuan died and Emperor Cheng took the throne, the new sovereign held Wang Shang in high regard and appointed him General of the Left.

sentences.zh: 而帝元舅大司馬大將軍王鳳顓權，行多驕僭。

translations.literal: But the emperor's eldest uncle on the mother's side, Grand Marshal and Grand General Wang Feng, monopolized power, and his conduct was often arrogant and presumptuous.
translations.idiomatic: Meanwhile Wang Feng, the emperor's senior maternal uncle, served as grand marshal and grand general and dominated the government, and his behavior was frequently overbearing and out of line.

sentences.zh: 商論議不能平鳳，鳳知之，亦疏商。

translations.literal: In debate Shang could not make Feng submit; Feng knew it and also kept Shang at a distance.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Shang could never win a policy argument against Wang Feng; Feng noticed the tension and grew cold toward him.

sentences.zh: 建始三年秋，京師民無故相驚，言大水至，百姓奔走相蹂躪、老弱號呼，長安中大亂。

translations.literal: In the autumn of the third year of the Jianshi era, the people of the capital for no reason frightened one another, saying great floodwaters were coming; the common people ran about, trampling one another, the old and weak cried out, and great chaos arose in Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: In the autumn of Jianshi 3, rumor swept the capital that a catastrophic flood was imminent; people panicked, stampeded, and trampled one another while the elderly and children wailed, and Chang'an descended into chaos.

sentences.zh: 天子親御前殿，召公卿議。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven personally went to the front hall and summoned the dukes and ministers to deliberate.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor went in person to the front hall and called the high ministers into council.

sentences.zh: 大將軍鳳以為太后與上及後宮可御船，令吏民上長安城以避水。

translations.literal: Grand General Feng thought the empress dowager, the sovereign, and the rear palace could board boats, and ordered clerks and people to ascend the walls of Chang'an to escape the water.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Feng proposed putting the empress dowager, the emperor, and the harem aboard boats and ordering officials and townsfolk onto the ramparts of Chang'an to ride out the supposed flood.

sentences.zh: 群臣皆從鳳議。

translations.literal: The host of ministers all followed Feng's proposal.
translations.idiomatic: The entire court fell in behind Wang Feng's plan.

sentences.zh: 左將軍商獨曰：「自古無道之國，水猶不冒城郭。

translations.literal: General of the Left Shang alone said, 'Since antiquity, even in states without the Way, floodwaters have not overrun city walls.'
translations.idiomatic: Only General of the Left Wang Shang objected: 'Even under the worst rulers in history, rivers have not simply swallowed whole capitals overnight.'

sentences.zh: 今政治和平，世無兵革，上下相安，何因當有大水一日暴至？

translations.literal: Now government and policies are peaceful, the age has no warfare, high and low are at ease with one another—what reason could there be for great waters suddenly to arrive in a single day?
translations.idiomatic: We live in a time of peace, without war, and ruler and ruled are secure together; what could cause a deluge to strike the capital in an instant?

sentences.zh: 此必訛言也，不宜令上城，重驚百姓。」

translations.literal: This must be false rumor; it is not fitting to order people onto the walls and again frighten the hundred surnames.'
translations.idiomatic: This is surely a groundless scare. Ordering everyone onto the walls would only panic the populace again.'

sentences.zh: 上乃止。

translations.literal: The sovereign thereupon stopped.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor dropped the idea.

sentences.zh: 有頃，長安中稍定，問之，果訛言。

translations.literal: After a while, within Chang'an things gradually settled; when it was investigated, it was indeed false rumor.
translations.idiomatic: Before long calm returned to Chang'an, and inquiries confirmed that the alarm had been nothing but rumor.

sentences.zh: 上於是美壯商之固守，數稱其議。

translations.literal: The sovereign thereupon praised and admired Shang's firm guarding, and repeatedly praised his proposal.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then admired Wang Shang's steadiness under pressure and repeatedly commended his counsel.

sentences.zh: 而鳳大慚，自恨失言。

translations.literal: But Feng was greatly ashamed and hated himself for having misspoken.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Feng was deeply embarrassed and bitterly regretted his rash words.

sentences.zh: 明年，商代匡衡為丞相，益封千戶，天子甚尊任之。

translations.literal: The next year, Shang replaced Kuang Heng as chancellor; his fief was increased by a thousand households, and the Son of Heaven greatly honored and employed him.
translations.idiomatic: The following year Wang Shang succeeded Kuang Heng as chancellor, received an additional thousand-household grant to his estate, and enjoyed the emperor's full confidence.

sentences.zh: 為人多質有威重，長八尺餘，身體鴻大，容貌甚過絕人。

translations.literal: As a person he was much of substance and possessed awe-inspiring dignity; he was more than eight chi in height, his body was vast, and his appearance greatly surpassed other men.
translations.idiomatic: He was plainspoken yet imposing—well over eight feet tall, with a massive frame and a face that towered above ordinary men.

sentences.zh: 河平四年，單于來朝，引見白虎殿。

translations.literal: In the fourth year of the Heping era, the Chanyu came to court and was led in audience at the White Tiger Hall.
translations.idiomatic: In Heping 4 the Xiongnu chanyu paid a formal visit, and the court received him in the White Tiger Hall.

sentences.zh: 丞相商坐未央廷中，單于前，拜謁商。

translations.literal: Chancellor Shang sat in the Weiyang court; the Chanyu advanced and bowed in audience to Shang.
translations.idiomatic: Chancellor Wang Shang took his seat in the Weiyang Palace court; the chanyu stepped forward and performed obeisance to him.

sentences.zh: 商起，離席與言，單于仰視商貌，大畏之，遷延卻退。

translations.literal: Shang rose, left his mat, and spoke with him; the Chanyu looked up at Shang's appearance, greatly feared him, and hesitatingly drew back.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Shang stood, stepped from his place, and addressed him; when the chanyu lifted his eyes to Wang Shang's towering figure, he shrank back in awe and edged away.

sentences.zh: 天子聞而歎曰：「此真漢相矣！」

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven heard and sighed, saying, 'This is truly a Han chancellor!'
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor heard of it, he exclaimed, 'There speaks a true chancellor of Han!'

sentences.zh: 初，大將軍鳳連昏楊肜為琅邪太守，其郡有災害十四，已上。

translations.literal: Earlier, Grand General Feng had connected marriage with Yang Rong and made him governor of Langye; that commandery had fourteen or more disasters.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Feng had earlier married into the family of Yang Rong and installed him as governor of Langye, where more than a dozen calamities went unaddressed.

sentences.zh: 商部屬按問，鳳以曉商曰：「災異天事，非人力所為。

translations.literal: Shang's subordinate officials investigated; Feng used it to inform Shang, saying, 'Disasters and prodigies are affairs of Heaven, not what human power does.'
translations.idiomatic: Wang Shang ordered his staff to look into the matter. Wang Feng sent word: 'Portents come from Heaven; no mortal official controls them.'

sentences.zh: 肜素善吏，宜以為後。」

translations.literal: Rong has always been good as an official; he should be considered for later.'
translations.idiomatic: Yang Rong has always been capable in office; you ought to go easy on him.'

sentences.zh: 商不聽，竟奏免肜，奏果寢不下，鳳重以是怨商，陰求其短，使人上書言商閨門內事。

translations.literal: Shang did not listen; in the end he memorialized to dismiss Rong; the memorial indeed lay asleep and was not issued; Feng for this reason again resented Shang, secretly sought his shortcomings, and caused someone to submit a memorial speaking of affairs inside Shang's women's quarters.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Shang ignored the hint, memorialized for Yang Rong's removal, and saw the document shelved. Wang Feng's resentment deepened; he began hunting for dirt and had someone memorialize about scandal inside Wang Shang's household.

sentences.zh: 天子以為暗昧之過，不足以傷大臣，鳳固爭，下其事司隸。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven thought obscure faults were not sufficient to injure a great minister; Feng firmly disputed, and the matter was sent down to the metropolitan commandant.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor treated the charges as private indiscretions unworthy of ruining a senior minister, but Wang Feng pressed the case until the matter was referred to the metropolitan superintendent.

sentences.zh: 先是，皇太后嘗詔問商女，欲以備後宮。

translations.literal: Before this, the empress dowager had once issued an edict inquiring about Shang's daughter, wishing to prepare her for the rear palace.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier the empress dowager had formally inquired after Wang Shang's daughter with an eye to admitting her to the harem.

sentences.zh: 時女病，商意亦難之，以病對，不入。

translations.literal: At that time the daughter was ill; Shang in his mind also found it difficult; he answered with illness, and she did not enter.
translations.idiomatic: The girl happened to be ill, and Wang Shang himself was reluctant; he pleaded sickness, and she never entered the palace.

sentences.zh: 及商以閨門事見考，自知為鳳所中，惶怖，更欲內女為援，乃因新幸李婕妤家白見其女。

translations.literal: When Shang because of women's-quarters matters underwent examination, he knew himself to have been struck by Feng; frightened, he further wished to send in a daughter as support; then through the household of the newly favored Lady Li, Jieyu, he reported and showed his daughter.
translations.idiomatic: Once Wang Shang faced investigation over domestic scandal, he realized Wang Feng had set him up. In panic he tried to push his daughter into the palace as a counterweight and used the household of the newly favored Lady Li to present her.

sentences.zh: 於是左將軍丹等奏：「商位三公，爵列侯，親受詔策為天下師，不遵法度以翼國家，而回辟下媚以進其私，執左道以亂政，為臣不忠，罔上不道，《甫刑》之辟，皆為上戮，罪名明白。

translations.literal: Thereupon General of the Left Dan and others submitted: 'Shang occupies the Three Excellencies, his noble rank is marquis; he personally received edicts and appointments as teacher to all under heaven; he does not follow laws and regulations in order to wing the state, but turns aside and flatters those below to advance his private interests, holds to the left path and disorders government; as a minister he is disloyal, deceives the sovereign and is not in accord with the Way; the punishments of the Fu Xing are all capital executions for superiors—his guilt is clear.'
translations.idiomatic: General of the Left Shi Dan and his colleagues then memorialized: 'Wang Shang ranks among the Three Excellencies and holds a marquisate; he was personally commissioned to guide the realm. Instead of upholding the law to support the state, he courts favor below and pursues private ends, peddling heterodox practices that corrupt government. Such disloyalty and deceit of the throne violate the standards of the Fu Xing and merit execution; his offenses are plain.'

sentences.zh: 臣請詔謁者召商詣若盧詔獄。」

translations.literal: We request that an edict be issued to the usher summoning Shang to the Rolu imperial prison.'
translations.idiomatic: We ask that Your Majesty order the court usher to take Wang Shang into custody at the Rolu imperial prison.'

sentences.zh: 上素重商，知匡言多險，制曰「勿治」。

translations.literal: The sovereign had always esteemed Shang; he knew Kuang's words were mostly treacherous, and issued a rescript saying, 'Do not prosecute.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor had long respected Wang Shang and recognized Zhang Kuang's memorial for the vicious attack it was, so he ruled, 'Take no further action.'

sentences.zh: 鳳固爭之，於是制詔御史：「蓋丞相以德輔翼國家，典領百寮，協和萬國，為職任莫重焉。

translations.literal: Feng firmly disputed it; thereupon an edict was issued to the imperial secretary: 'In general the chancellor with virtue assists and wings the state, presides over the host of officials, harmonizes the myriad states—of offices and duties none is heavier.'
translations.idiomatic: Wang Feng would not let the matter drop, and the emperor had to issue an edict to the censor-in-chief: 'The chancellor exists to support the dynasty with moral authority, to head the bureaucracy, and to keep the realm in harmony—no burden is weightier.'

sentences.zh: 今樂昌侯商為丞相，出入五年，未聞忠言嘉謀，而有不忠執左道之辜，陷於大辟。

translations.literal: Now Lechang Marquis Shang has been chancellor, going in and out five years; loyal words and fine plans have not been heard from him, yet there is the guilt of disloyalty and holding to the left path, falling into great execution.
translations.idiomatic: Yet in five years as chancellor, Lechang Marquis Wang Shang has offered no loyal counsel worthy of the name, while the charge of disloyalty and heterodox conduct would carry the death penalty.'

sentences.zh: 前商女弟內行不修，奴賊殺人，疑商教使，為商重臣，故抑而不窮。

translations.literal: Earlier Shang's younger sister's conduct within was not cultivated; a slave villainously killed a person; there was suspicion Shang instructed and sent him; because Shang was a weighty minister, therefore it was suppressed and not fully investigated.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier his younger sister's private conduct was scandalous: a household slave committed murder, and some suspected Wang Shang ordered it. Because he was a pillar of the court, I suppressed the case and did not press to the bottom.'

sentences.zh: 今或言商不以自悔而反怨懟，朕甚傷之。

translations.literal: Now some say Shang does not thereby repent himself but instead harbors resentment; We are greatly wounded by it.'
translations.idiomatic: Now I am told he shows no remorse but nurses grievance instead. That wounds me deeply.'

sentences.zh: 惟商與先帝有外親，未忍致於理。

translations.literal: Only, Shang has outer kinship with the late emperor; We have not been able to bear bringing him to the law.'
translations.idiomatic: Yet Wang Shang is tied to the late emperor by marriage, and I cannot bring myself to subject him to full criminal process.'

sentences.zh: 其赦商罪。

translations.literal: Let Shang's crimes be pardoned.'
translations.idiomatic: I therefore pardon his offenses.'

sentences.zh: 使者收丞相印綬。」

translations.literal: Let envoys collect the chancellor's seal and ribbon.'
translations.idiomatic: Envoys shall relieve him of the chancellor's seal and sash.'

sentences.zh: 商死後，連年日蝕、地震，直臣京兆尹王章上封事召開，訟商忠直無罪，言鳳顓權蔽主。

translations.literal: After Shang died, year after year there were solar eclipses and earthquakes; the upright official metropolitan governor Wang Zhang submitted a sealed memorial when summoned to audience, arguing Shang was loyal and straight and without guilt, saying Feng monopolized power and blocked the ruler.
translations.idiomatic: After Wang Shang's death, solar eclipses and earthquakes followed year on year. Wang Zhang, the principled governor of the capital, filed a sealed memorial; when the emperor opened the matter, Zhang argued that Wang Shang had been loyal and blameless and that Wang Feng had monopolized power and blinded the throne.

sentences.zh: 鳳竟以法誅章，語在《元後傳》。

translations.literal: Feng in the end by law executed Zhang; the account is in the 'Biography of the Yuan Empress Dowager.'
translations.idiomatic: Wang Feng eventually had Wang Zhang executed on a legal pretext; the story is told in the biography of Empress Dowager Yuan.

sentences.zh: 至元始中，王莽為安漢公，誅不附己者，樂昌侯安見被以罪，自殺，國除。

translations.literal: By the Yuanshi era, Wang Mang was Duke of Anhan; he executed those who did not attach to himself; Lechang Marquis An was seen to be charged with crime, killed himself, and the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: In the Yuanshi years Wang Mang, as Duke of Anhan, purged everyone who would not bend to him. Lechang Marquis Wang An was framed, took his own life, and his marquisate was extinguished.

sentences.zh: 自元帝為太子時，丹以父高任為中庶子，侍從十餘年。

translations.literal: From the time when Emperor Yuan was heir apparent, Dan because of his father Gao's appointment served as palace attendant to the heir, attending and following more than ten years.
translations.idiomatic: While Yuan was still crown prince, Shi Dan secured a junior tutor's post through his father's rank and attended the heir for more than a decade.

sentences.zh: 元帝即位，為駙馬都尉侍中，出常驂乘，甚有寵。

translations.literal: When Emperor Yuan ascended the throne, he became chief commandant of attendants cavalry and palace attendant; when going out he often rode in the accompanying chariot; he had great favor.
translations.idiomatic: When Yuan took the throne, Shi Dan became chief commandant of the consort cavalry and palace attendant, routinely rode beside the emperor in the side car, and basked in imperial favor.

sentences.zh: 上以丹舊臣，皇考外屬，親信之，詔丹護太子家。

translations.literal: The sovereign because Dan was an old minister and outer kin of the imperial father trusted him closely, and issued an edict for Dan to guard the heir apparent's household.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor treated Shi Dan as a veteran of his father's household and a maternal relative, kept him close, and ordered him to oversee the crown prince's establishment.

sentences.zh: 是時，傅昭儀子定陶共王有材藝，子母俱愛幸，而太子頗有酒色之失，母王皇后無寵。

translations.literal: At this time, Consort Fu's son the Prince of Dingtao, the Prince of Gong, had talent and arts; son and mother were both loved and favored, while the heir apparent had quite failings of wine and sex, and his mother Empress Wang had no favor.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Consort Fu's son, Prince Kang of Dingtao, was gifted and accomplished, and mother and son both enjoyed the emperor's affection, whereas the crown prince showed a weakness for wine and women and his mother, Empress Wang, had fallen out of favor.

sentences.zh: 丹為人足知，愷弟愛人，貌若儻蕩不備，然心甚謹密，故尤得信於上。

translations.literal: Dan as a person was sufficient in knowledge, kindly and fraternal, loving people; his appearance seemed careless and unprepared, yet his heart was very careful and secret; therefore he especially obtained trust from the sovereign.
translations.idiomatic: Shi Dan was shrewd, warm, and considerate; he looked relaxed and even careless, but he was meticulous by nature, which is why the emperor trusted him above others.

sentences.zh: 丹兄嗣父爵為侯，讓不受分。

translations.literal: Dan's elder brother inherited the father's noble rank as marquis and yielded, not accepting division.
translations.idiomatic: His elder brother inherited their father's marquisate; Shi Dan declined any split of the estate.

sentences.zh: 丹盡得父財，身又食大國邑，重以舊恩，數見褒賞，賞賜累千金，僮奴以百數，後房妻妾數十人，內奢淫，好飲酒，極滋味聲色之樂。

translations.literal: Dan completely obtained the father's wealth; his person moreover ate a great state's fief; doubled with old favor, he repeatedly received praise and rewards; rewards and gifts accumulated to a thousand pounds of gold; household slaves were in the hundreds; rear chambers had wives and concubines in the tens of persons; within he was extravagant and dissolute, fond of drinking wine, and exhausted the pleasures of flavors, sounds, and sex.
translations.idiomatic: He kept the whole of his father's fortune, drew income from a large fief, and, thanks to long-standing favor, was heaped with honors and gifts worth thousands in gold. He kept hundreds of slaves, dozens of concubines, and lived in private luxury—drinking heavily and indulging every appetite for food, music, and women.

sentences.zh: 為將軍前後十六年，永始中病乞骸骨，上賜策曰：「左將軍寢病不衰，願歸治疾，朕愍以官職之事久留將軍，使躬不瘳。

translations.literal: Serving as general before and after sixteen years, in the Yongshi era he was ill and begged for his bones; the sovereign bestowed a rescript saying: 'General of the Left, your lying illness does not wane; you wish to return to treat illness; We pity that the affairs of office have long detained the general, causing your person not to recover.'
translations.idiomatic: He served sixteen years as a general. In the Yongshi period he fell ill and asked to retire. The emperor replied: 'Your illness as General of the Left does not improve, and you wish to go home to heal. I regret that state business has kept you at your post so long that your health has not mended.'

sentences.zh: 使光祿勳賜將軍黃金五十斤，安車駟馬，其上將軍印綬。

translations.literal: Let the superintendent of the imperial household be sent to bestow on the general fifty jin of gold, a secure carriage and four horses; let him present the general's seal and ribbon.'
translations.idiomatic: Send the superintendent of the imperial household with fifty catties of gold, a comfortable carriage and team of four, and receive your seal and sash as general.'

sentences.zh: 宜專精神，務近醫藥，以輔不衰。」

translations.literal: You should concentrate spirit, devote yourself to drawing near to physicians and medicine, in order to assist non-declining.'
translations.idiomatic: Devote yourself to rest and medical care so that your strength may return.'

sentences.zh: 大司空何武、尚書令唐林皆上書言：「喜行義修潔，忠誠憂國，內輔之臣也，今以寢病，一旦遣歸，眾庶失望，皆曰傅氏賢子，以論議不合於定陶太后故退，百寮莫不為國恨之。

translations.literal: Grand Minister of Works He Wu and secretary director Tang Lin both submitted memorials saying: 'Xi in conduct and righteousness is cultivated and pure, loyal and sincere, worrying for the state; he is a minister of inner assistance; now because of lying illness, in one morning sent back, the multitude will be disappointed; all will say the Fu family's worthy son, because discussions did not accord with the Dowager of Dingtao therefore retired; the hundred officials none do not for the state resent it.'
translations.idiomatic: Grand Minister of Works He Wu and Secretary Director Tang Lin jointly wrote: 'Fu Xi is upright, scrupulous, and devoted to the state—exactly the kind of man who should stand at the emperor's side. To dismiss him overnight on grounds of illness will disappoint the empire. People will say the worthy son of the Fu clan was cashiered because he would not trim his opinions to suit the Dowager of Dingtao, and every official will feel the dynasty has wronged itself.'

sentences.zh: 忠臣，社稷之衛，魯以季友治亂，楚以子玉輕重，魏以無忌折衝，項以范增存亡。

translations.literal: Loyal ministers are the bulwark of altars of soil and grain; Lu by Jiyou ordered chaos; Chu by Ziyu had weight light or heavy; Wei by Wuji repelled the enemy; Xiang by Fan Zeng existed or perished.
translations.idiomatic: Loyal ministers are what defend the altars of state: Lu rose or fell with Jiyou, Chu with Ziyu, Wei with Lord Xinling, and Xiang Yu with Fan Zeng.'

sentences.zh: 故楚跨有南土，帶甲百萬，鄰國不以為難，子玉為將，則文公側席而坐，及其死也，君臣相慶。

translations.literal: Therefore Chu straddled and possessed the southern lands, belt-armor a million; neighboring states did not regard it as difficult; when Ziyu was general, then Duke Wen sat sideways on the mat; when he died, ruler and ministers mutually rejoiced.
translations.idiomatic: Chu commanded the south with a million armored men, yet its neighbors did not fear it—until Ziyu took command, when Duke Wen of Jin could not sit at ease. The moment Ziyu died, ruler and ministers across the river celebrated.'

sentences.zh: 百萬之眾，不如一賢，故秦行千金以間廉頗，漢散萬金以疏亞父。

translations.literal: A host of a million is not equal to one worthy; therefore Qin employed a thousand pounds of gold to alienate Lian Po, and Han scattered ten thousand pounds of gold to separate the Sire Ya.'
translations.idiomatic: A million soldiers count for less than one wise counselor—hence Qin spent a thousand in gold to drive a wedge beside Lian Po, and Han poured out ten thousand to estrange Fan Zeng from his lord.'

sentences.zh: 喜立於朝，陛下之光輝，傅氏之廢興也。」

translations.literal: Xi standing at court is Your Majesty's glory and brightness, and the Fu family's ruin or rise.'
translations.idiomatic: Fu Xi's presence at court is both your majesty's good fortune and the making or breaking of the Fu clan.'

sentences.zh: 上亦自重之。

translations.literal: The sovereign also on his own valued him.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor himself still set great store by him.

sentences.zh: 明年正月，乃徙師丹為大司空，而拜喜為大司馬，封高武侯。

translations.literal: In the first month of the next year, Shi Dan was transferred to the office of grand minister of works, and Xi was appointed grand marshal and enfeoffed as Marquis of Gaowu.
translations.idiomatic: The following first month the emperor moved Shi Dan into the grand minister of works post, appointed Fu Xi grand marshal, and enfeoffed him as Marquis of Gaowu.

sentences.zh: 丁、傅驕奢，皆嫉喜之恭儉。

translations.literal: Ding and Fu were arrogant and extravagant; all envied Xi's respectfulness and frugality.
translations.idiomatic: The Ding and Fu factions lived in swaggering luxury and resented Fu Xi's modest restraint.

sentences.zh: 又傅太后欲求稱尊號，與成帝母齊尊，喜與丞相孔光、大司空師丹共執正議。

translations.literal: Moreover the Fu empress dowager wished to seek to be called with an honored title, equal in honor with Emperor Cheng's mother; Xi together with Chancellor Kong Guang and Grand Minister of Works Shi Dan jointly upheld correct deliberation.
translations.idiomatic: When Grand Empress Dowager Fu demanded a title equal to Emperor Cheng's mother, Fu Xi stood with Chancellor Kong Guang and Grand Minister of Works Shi Dan on the side of precedent.

sentences.zh: 傅太后大怒，上不得已，先免師丹以感動喜，喜終不順。

translations.literal: The Fu empress dowager was greatly angry; the sovereign had no choice; first he dismissed Shi Dan in order to move Xi; Xi in the end did not comply.
translations.idiomatic: The grand empress dowager raged. Cornered, the emperor first dismissed Shi Dan, hoping to intimidate Fu Xi into yielding; Fu Xi still refused to bend.

sentences.zh: 後數月，遂策免喜曰：「君輔政出入三年，未有昭然匡朕不逮，而本朝大臣遂其奸心，咎由君焉。

translations.literal: After several months, then by edict he dismissed Xi, saying: 'Lord, in assisting government going in and out three years, there has not been clear correction of Our not reaching, while ministers of this court have accomplished their treacherous hearts—the fault is from the lord.'
translations.idiomatic: Months later an edict stripped him of office: 'You have held power for three years without once plainly correcting my shortcomings, while intriguers in this court have had their way. The blame rests with you.'

sentences.zh: 其上大司馬印綬，就第。」

translations.literal: Present the grand marshal's seal and ribbon; go to your mansion.'
translations.idiomatic: Surrender the grand marshal's seal and sash and retire to your estate.'

sentences.zh: 傅太后又自詔丞相、御史曰：「高武侯喜無功而封，內懷不忠，附下罔上，與故大司空丹同心背畔，放命圮族，虧損德化，罪惡雖在赦前，不宜奉朝請，其遣就國。」

translations.literal: The Fu empress dowager again herself edicted the chancellor and imperial secretary, saying: 'Gaowu Marquis Xi was enfeoffed without merit; within he harbors disloyalty, attaches to those below and deceives those above; with the former grand minister of works Dan of one mind he turned his back and rebelled; he abandoned commands and ruined the clan; he damaged and diminished virtuous transformation; although crimes and evils were before the amnesty, it is not fitting that he attend court audience; let him be sent to his state.'
translations.idiomatic: Grand Empress Dowager Fu then issued her own order to the chancellor and censor: 'Marquis of Gaowu Fu Xi received a fief without merit. In his heart he is disloyal: he panders to factions below while misleading the throne, and he conspired with the former grand minister of works Shi Dan in mutinous defiance. Such conduct tears down the moral order. Even though his offenses predate the last amnesty, he must not continue at court; send him back to his fief.'

sentences.zh: 後又欲奪喜侯，上亦不聽。

translations.literal: Later she again wished to strip Xi of his marquisate; the sovereign also did not listen.
translations.idiomatic: She later tried to strip him of his title as well, but the emperor would not allow it.

sentences.zh: 喜在國三歲余，哀帝崩，平帝即位，王莽用事，免傅氏宮爵歸故郡，晏將妻子徙合浦。

translations.literal: Xi was in his state more than three years; Emperor Ai died; Emperor Ping ascended the throne; Wang Mang handled affairs; the Fu clan's palace ranks were dismissed and they returned to their old commanderies; Yan led wife and children and moved to Hepu.
translations.idiomatic: Fu Xi spent three years in his fief. After Emperor Ai died and the child Emperor Ping ascended, Wang Mang took charge, stripped the Fu clan of their titles, and sent them home; Ding Yan removed his family to Hepu on the southern coast.

sentences.zh: 莽白太后下詔曰：「高武侯喜姿性端愨，論議忠直。

translations.literal: Mang informed the empress dowager and issued an edict saying: 'Gaowu Marquis Xi in bearing and nature is upright and careful; in discussion and deliberation loyal and straight.'
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang persuaded the empress dowager to issue an edict: 'Marquis of Gaowu Fu Xi is sober, steady, and outspoken in loyalty.'

sentences.zh: 雖與故定陶太后有屬，終不順指從邪，介然守節，以故斥逐就國。

translations.literal: Although he had kinship with the former Dowager of Dingtao, in the end he did not follow finger-pointing and follow evil; firmly he guarded integrity; for this reason he was expelled and sent to his state.'
translations.idiomatic: Though related to the late Dowager of Dingtao, he never truckled to her whims; he held his integrity and was banished to his fief for it.'

sentences.zh: 傳不雲乎？

translations.literal: Does the tradition not say?
translations.idiomatic: Has not the classic said,'

sentences.zh: 『歲寒然後知松伯之後凋也』。

translations.literal: "Only when the year is cold do we know that the pine and cypress are the last to wither."'
translations.idiomatic: Only when the year turns bitter do we see which pines and cypresses keep their green last'?"

sentences.zh: 其還喜長安，以故高安侯莫府賜喜，位特進，奉朝請。」

translations.literal: Let Xi be returned to Chang'an; with the former Gao'an Marquis's headquarters grant Xi; rank specially advanced; attend court audience.'
translations.idiomatic: Recall Fu Xi to Chang'an, grant him the former Gao'an Marquis's headquarters as a residence, advance him to specially advanced rank, and permit him to attend scheduled audiences.'

sentences.zh: 喜雖外見褒賞，孤立憂懼，後復遣就國，以壽終。

translations.literal: Although Xi outwardly received praise and rewards, he stood alone in worry and fear; later again he was sent to his state, and ended his life by old age.
translations.idiomatic: Outwardly honored, Fu Xi lived in isolation and dread, was sent back to his fief a second time, and died there in old age.

sentences.zh: 莽賜謚曰貞侯。

translations.literal: Mang bestowed posthumous name Marquis Zhen.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang gave him the posthumous title Marquis of Integrity.

sentences.zh: 子嗣，莽敗乃絕。

translations.literal: A son succeeded; when Mang was defeated, then it was cut off.
translations.idiomatic: His son inherited the line, which ended when Wang Mang fell.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：自宜、元、成、哀外戚興者，許、史、三王、丁、傅之家，皆重侯累將，窮貴極富，見其位矣，未見其人也。

translations.literal: The appraiser says: From Emperor Yi, Yuan, Cheng, and Ai, those by whom outer kin rose—the houses of Xu, Shi, the three Wangs, Ding, and Fu—all piled marquisates and repeated generals, exhausted nobility and exhausted wealth; one sees their positions, one has not seen their persons.
translations.idiomatic: The historian's judgment: From the reigns of Yi through Ai, the consort clans—the Xu, the Shi, the three Wang lines, the Ding, and the Fu—stacked marquisates and generalships until they towered in wealth and rank; you could see their offices, rarely the men who deserved them.

sentences.zh: 陽平之王多有材能，好事慕名，其勢尤盛，曠貴最久。

translations.literal: The Wangs of Yangping mostly had talent and ability, loved affairs and sought fame; their power was especially flourishing; they were long in noble rank the longest.
translations.idiomatic: The Yangping Wangs were unusually able, ambitious, and conspicuous; no other kin clan held such power for so long.

sentences.zh: 然至於莽，亦以覆國。

translations.literal: Yet reaching to Mang, thereby also overturned the state.
translations.idiomatic: Yet it was that same house that produced Wang Mang and overturned the dynasty.

sentences.zh: 王商有剛毅節，廢黜以憂死，非其罪也。

translations.literal: Wang Shang had firm and resolute integrity; dismissed and demoted, he died of worry; it was not his crime.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Shang was a man of iron principle; driven from office, he died of grief for a fault that was not his own.

sentences.zh: 史丹父子相繼，高以重厚，位至三公。

translations.literal: Shi Gao and Shi Dan, father and son, succeeded one another in office; Gao by weighty steadiness, their rank reached the Three Excellencies.
translations.idiomatic: Shi Gao and his son Shi Dan followed each other into high office; the father was famed for his sober reliability, and both rose to the Three Excellencies.

sentences.zh: 丹之輔道副主，掩惡揚美，傅會善意，雖宿儒達士無以加焉。

translations.literal: Dan's assisting and guiding the deputy lord, covering evil and raising beauty, attaching and joining good intentions—even old Confucians and penetrating scholars had nothing by which to add thereto.
translations.idiomatic: In guiding the heir he glossed over faults, magnified virtues, and put the best face on the prince's intentions—skills no veteran scholar could have surpassed.

sentences.zh: 及其歷房闥，入臥內，推至誠，犯顏色，動寤萬乘，轉移大謀，卒成太子，安母后之位。

translations.literal: Reaching to when he passed through the bedchamber doors, entered the inner sleeping quarters, pushed utmost sincerity, violated facial expression, moved and awakened the ten thousand chariots, shifted great plans, in the end completed the heir apparent, secured the empress dowager's position.
translations.idiomatic: When he crossed into the inner apartments, spoke with blunt sincerity, and risked the emperor's anger, he swayed a decision that touched the throne itself, secured the succession for the crown prince, and preserved the empress's station.

sentences.zh: 「無言不讎」，終獲忠貞之報。

translations.literal: 'No word spoken without answer'—in the end he obtained the reward of loyalty and steadfastness.
translations.idiomatic: The ode says, 'No loyal word goes unanswered'—and he lived to see the reward of his constancy.

sentences.zh: 傅喜守節不傾，亦蒙後凋之賞。

translations.literal: Fu Xi guarded integrity and did not lean; also received the reward of later withering.
translations.idiomatic: Fu Xi held his integrity without wavering and likewise earned the praise reserved for the last evergreen.

sentences.zh: 哀、平際會，禍福速哉！

translations.literal: At the juncture meeting of Ai and Ping—misfortune and fortune swift!
translations.idiomatic: How swiftly fortune turned between the reigns of Ai and Ping!
